decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). Previous attempts to increase spreader conductivity hit the upper bound (500.0). Following the strategy shift suggestion, I will now apply a coordinated geometric change by increasing the heat spreader width to improve lateral heat distribution towards the cooled boundaries.

changes.reason: Increasing the spreader width expands the high-conductivity path laterally, allowing heat to reach the left and right Dirichlet boundaries more effectively, thereby reducing the peak temperature in the chip.

expected_effects: More uniform temperature distribution across the top layer
Improved heat extraction efficiency via existing boundary conditions
risk_notes: Widening the spreader approaches the design domain width limit (1.2m), leaving less margin for future horizontal expansion.
If the spreader becomes too wide relative to the heat source location without sufficient vertical coupling, diminishing returns may occur, though unlikely at this scale.
